title: chrysopteris aurea
intro: Chrysopteris aurea, also known as the golden club moss, is a type of plant that belongs to the lycopodiaceae family and is native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties that can help to soothe skin irritations and wounds. The health benefits of Chrysopteris aurea include its potential to reduce fever, alleviate respiratory issues, and support the immune system. Chrysopteris aurea is typically prepared as a tea or decoction, and it can also be used in topical applications, such as creams and salves, to take advantage of its medicinal properties.
